conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that the drive was stressful. Can you tell me a little bit about your daily routine? What's been going on at work?
Yeah, okay, so I've just been trying to get through each day without too much trouble. Work's been pretty tough lately, but I'm trying not to think about it.
It sounds like you're trying to cope with some stressors. How have you been sleeping?
Honestly, sleep has been a real struggle. I've been waking up in the middle of the night and having trouble falling back asleep.
That can be really frustrating. Have there been any particular events or situations that might be contributing to your sleep issues?
Yeah, work's been pretty demanding lately. We're getting ready for some big cases coming up, and I feel like I'm shouldering a lot of the responsibility.
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed. How have you been handling things at home? Do you have any hobbies or interests that bring you joy?
Yeah, I mean...I used to enjoy playing basketball, but lately, it's just been too hard to muster up the energy for it.
That can be tough when we lose our sense of pleasure in activities. Can you tell me more about what's been going on at home? Are there any relationships that are important to you?
Yeah, I have a pretty good friend who's been trying to check in on me, but I've just been pushing her away.
It sounds like you might be feeling like you're not wanting to burden others with your problems. How about your appetite? Have you noticed any changes there?
Actually, my appetite has been pretty off too. I mean, I'll eat when I need to, but it's just not something that excites me anymore.
It sounds like you're feeling disconnected from a lot of things right now. Are there any particular times of the day or situations where you start to feel better?
Yeah, actually, sometimes in the morning, when I'm getting ready for work, I'll listen to some music and it helps take my mind off things.
That's great that you've found something that gives you a sense of relief. Have there been any recent stressful events or changes in your life?
Yeah, just the usual stuff – people trying to get me to do more at work, dealing with some personal issues...nothing too crazy.
It sounds like you're just feeling overwhelmed by all these demands. How have you been handling those stressors so far?
Honestly? I've just been trying to power through and avoid dealing with it all.
That can be exhausting, both physically and emotionally. What do you think is the biggest challenge for you right now?
Just getting through each day without losing my mind, I guess.
It sounds like you're really struggling to find a sense of purpose or meaning. Are there any small steps you could take towards making some changes in your life?
Yeah...I mean, maybe trying to prioritize self-care more? Taking care of myself?
That's a great start! Prioritizing self-care can be really powerful. What are some ways that you think you could start doing that?
Honestly, I'm not sure. Maybe just taking some time for myself each day, doing something I enjoy?
That sounds like a great place to start. Are there any specific goals or intentions you'd like to set for our next session?
Yeah...I guess just wanting to feel more in control of my life.
That's a great goal! We can definitely work on that. Is there anything else that's been on your mind lately?
Just the usual stuff – trying to get through each day, dealing with the guilt and shame...
It sounds like you're really struggling with some heavy emotions right now. How do you think we could start working on those feelings?
Honestly? I don't know...maybe just talking about it more?
That's a great place to start! We can definitely explore that further. What do you want to focus on for our next session?
Just wanting to feel better, I guess.
That's a great goal! We'll work towards that. Let's keep exploring some coping strategies and ways to prioritize self-care.
